Earlier that fateful night, 43-year-old Andrea Rosewich of Avon, Ohio, and 55-year-old Paul Prowant of Seattle, Washington, were struck by Stephanie's vehicle.
Sadly, there was no saving them.
Both victims were pronounced dead at the scene.

After news of Stephanie's arrest was made public, her fellow students at Bradley University held a candlelight vigil for the victims and later protested the university's decision to allow Stephanie to participate in the commencement.
Ultimately, she was not allowed to take part in the graduation ceremony, but her degree was conferred nonetheless.
Stephanie eventually admitted to striking both pedestrians and entered a blind plea to two counts of aggravated DUI and two counts of aggravated reckless driving.
In April of 2023, Stephanie was sentenced to 14 years in prison.
